actionable_feedback: Open the .xlsx in an environment with openpyxl (or equivalent) and validate every formula programmatically; attach parsing success logs or exported CSVs showing computed values.
Fix the malformed SUMPRODUCT expression in the memo/spreadsheet and ensure all formulas are syntactically valid Excel formulas.

Standardize variable names in the workbook to exactly match Schema column headers (e.g., stall_cycles, max_stall, fifo_full) and replace ambiguous temporary stand-ins (Cx, Bx, Dx) with explicit cell references or named ranges.
Demonstrate multiplicative application of percent-change remediations with explicit pre- and post-remediation calculations for at least one sample row per record; include the computed numeric values.
Add a budget breakdown table in the memo and workbook that calculates the cost per scenario and demonstrates that chosen recommendations meet the max_budget constraint.
Add row-level provenance citations in the spreadsheet linking each computed cell back to the embedded-packet source (e.g., a column with source record id and exact cell citations).
Clarify ambiguous policy items noted in the memo (integer counter policy, release objective threshold) or mark them with explicit assumption cells that the verifier can check.

Re-run deterministic precheck and the semantic checks, and include a short verifier checklist (openpyxl parsing OK, sample calculations validated, malformed formulas corrected) when submitting the revised attempt.

expert_memo.md: Documents formulas and checks but includes a syntactically malformed SUMPRODUCT expression and ambiguous variable names (Cx/Bx/Dx) that hinder automated verification.
